title: Claiming a Parent as a Dependent: What Qualifies
summary: The rules for claiming a parent as a dependent are specific, and understanding them can meaningfully affect a family's taxes.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Claiming a Parent as a Dependent: What Qualifies | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: The rules for claiming a parent as a dependent are specific, and understanding them can meaningfully affect a family's taxes.
whatYouNeedToKnow: A parent's gross income must fall below a specific annual threshold.
You generally must have provided more than half of their financial support.
A parent doesn't need to live with you to qualify, unlike some other dependent categories.

paragraphs: Unlike claiming a child, a parent doesn't need to live in your household to qualify as a dependent — but their gross income must fall below a specific yearly threshold (which changes periodically), and you generally need to have provided more than half of their total financial support for the year.

Multiple siblings splitting a parent's support can sometimes use a "multiple support agreement" to let one sibling claim the dependency, even if no single person provided over half the support alone — worth discussing with a tax professional if support is shared.

nextSteps: Check the parent's gross income against the current year's threshold.
Calculate the actual proportion of support you provided.
Ask a tax professional about multiple support agreements if support is shared among siblings.
disclaimer: General information, not tax advice — thresholds change yearly, and a tax professional can confirm current eligibility.
